items.blurb: A snowy little world on one A5 sheet — Narumi Suzuki's tiny villagers and animals, ready to wander across your journal pages.
Disco energy for your planner: Heisuke Kitazawa's characters bust moves across a clear A5 sheet, one peel-off groove at a time.
Confetti, momentum, and cheerfully sprinting characters — Heisuke Kitazawa turns a plain planner page into a tiny marathon of joy.
Journal decorating with training wheels: a grid notebook, cut-and-paste papers, stickers, and an idea booklet in Rei Kurahashi's retro cat-girl style.
Two metres of foil-kissed cats and girls on a clear roll — snip out whatever catches your eye, like an endless sticker buffet.
A tiny book that opens to four flavours of retro romance — Rei Kurahashi's cats and girls carrying your reminders with maximum charm.
Fold open this little book and four cat-filled sticky designs tumble out — Rei Kurahashi's retro felines reporting for note duty.
Retro girls in their best outfits, packed into a book-style sticky set — four designs for to-do lists that deserve a little fashion.
See-through stickers of Rei Kurahashi's lovestruck cats and girls — they melt into washi layers like they were always there.
Waterproof, transparent, extremely feline — Rei Kurahashi's cat-day stickers layer beautifully over spreads, cards, and gift wrap.
Rei Kurahashi's stylishly dressed girls and their cats, printed on clear PVC so they blend into whatever spread you're building.
Rei Kurahashi's retro girl-and-cat art floating on a clear red bookmark — the prettiest possible way to stop losing your place.
Rei Kurahashi's retro girl-and-cat art floating on a clear black bookmark — the prettiest possible way to stop losing your place.
Rei Kurahashi's retro girl-and-cat art floating on a clear pink bookmark — the prettiest possible way to stop losing your place.
Rei Kurahashi's retro girl-and-cat art floating on a clear blue bookmark — the prettiest possible way to stop losing your place.
Rei Kurahashi's retro girl-and-cat art floating on a clear yellow bookmark — the prettiest possible way to stop losing your place.
A square Maruman sketchbook wearing Rei Kurahashi's girl-and-cats cover — acid-free paper that welcomes pencil, pen, and watercolour alike.
Maruman craftsmanship meets Rei Kurahashi's black cat cover — a square croquis book begging to hold your doodles and big ideas.
Die-cut cats lounging in a flower-filled garden — 60 sheets of soft feline chaos for page-marking and tiny messages.
Cats (and one dog) strolling a nostalgic old-town street, die-cut into sticky notes that make reminders feel like a holiday.
Sticky notes shaped like cats mid-interruption — perfect for anyone whose to-do list is regularly sat on by a real one.
A pile of napping cats, die-cut into sticky notes — leave someone a message wrapped in a very good nap.
Cats pottering around a garden on a made-in-Japan postcard — honestly too cute to mail, so maybe frame it instead.
Everyday cat life, postcard-sized and made in Japan — a two-second way to make someone's mailbox the best part of their day.
A postcard of cats politely demanding playtime — send it to the friend who texts you cat videos at midnight.
Sleepy cats, one postcard, infinite coze — the correct stationery for a low-effort, high-warmth hello.
Washi-paper cat stickers frolicking through the garden — gentle, peelable joy for planners and journal spreads.
A washi sticker sheet of cats going about their small-town business — the daily commute has never looked this soft.
Playful washi cats angling for attention — stick them anywhere a page needs a little mischief.
Washi stickers of cats in various states of nap — for decorating spreads at the exact energy level you're feeling.
Foil-stamped penguins that catch the light as they waddle across your planner — small birds, big shimmer.
Flying squirrels gliding in gold foil — a sticker sheet that turns any journal page into a tiny, glittering forest canopy.
Robin the black cat plays through four music-themed memo designs — 120 thick, bleed-proof sheets from duo Kuroneko Design.
Little Teo and friends set everyday notes to music — a 120-sheet square memo pad in Kuroneko Design's jazzy cat world.
Mino washi memo sheets carrying Midori Asano's soft everyday scenes — sized for the kind of note you actually mean.
Teatime illustrations on real Mino washi — Midori Asano's gentle 3pm world, one thoughtful note at a time.
An antique shop of vintage furniture and tableware, sketched onto Mino washi memo sheets — notes with serious browsing-day energy.
Home-style Japanese dishes in soft coloured pencil, printed on washi memo sheets — comfort food for your correspondence.
The cult Rollbahn notebook, shrunk to keyring size in crisp white — cream grid paper and a strap so it never leaves your bag.
Delfonics' beloved Rollbahn, micro-sized and dressed in pastel purple — clip it to your bag for ideas that strike on the go.
Fifty-four gold-stamped flake stickers from eraser-stamp artist eric — a whole happy town, one glinting flake at a time.
Costumed characters and technicolour drinks share one clear A5 sheet — Yuki Itoi's juice bar, open for planner decoration.
Yuki Itoi dresses tiny characters in fruit costumes and sets them loose on a clear sticker sheet — five-a-day for your journal.
Soft animals and softer sweets from Noriyuki Echigawa, on a clear-backed A5 sheet that settles gently into any spread.
A slow day off with a cat, illustrated by Noriyuki Echigawa and sliced into gentle clear stickers for your journal.
A clear A5 sheet crowded with Tadashi Nishiwaki's grinning dogs — instant good-boy energy for planners and parcels.
Tadashi Nishiwaki's ode to the bakery: carb-happy characters on a transparent sticker sheet, best applied while hungry.
A pearl-shimmer micro Rollbahn with the same lovely cream grid paper inside — tiny notebook, luminous first impression.
A colour-shifting metallic micro Rollbahn that changes with the light — the flashiest way to carry a grocery list.
Full hologram cover, keyring strap, pocketable proportions — this micro Rollbahn refuses to be a boring notebook.
Butter-soft pastel yellow on Delfonics' micro Rollbahn — sunshine for your keychain, grid paper for your brain.
Minty pastel green wraps this keyring-sized Rollbahn — the calmest possible home for on-the-go scribbles.
A sky-soft pastel blue micro Rollbahn with a strap for your bag — quick thoughts deserve pretty housing.
Pastel pink, pocket-sized, permanently attached to your bag — the micro Rollbahn that makes note-taking a treat.
A see-through blue cover shows off the spiral and cream pages of this micro Rollbahn — jelly-bag energy, notebook function.
Fully transparent cover, classic Rollbahn insides — a micro notebook with nothing to hide and everything to jot.
Translucent green over Delfonics' famous grid paper — a keyring notebook that looks like a boiled sweet.
Clear pink jelly vibes on the outside, serious Delfonics grid paper on the inside — a micro notebook made for bag charms and big plans.
Ten metres of Peppoko's soft little faces on washi — tear off a strip of gentle expressions wherever a page feels lonely.
Peppoko's daydreamy girls parade down a 10-metre washi roll — border your spreads with the softest company imaginable.
A hundred roll-style sticky notes in four Peppoko designs — soft bears and girls quietly upgrading your reminder game.
Peppoko's best pals share four sticky-roll designs — 100 sheets for notes you'd actually be happy to find on your desk.
Blooms on every sheet: Peppoko's flower-strewn sticky rolls make even a milk-run reminder look like a love letter.
A 1.2cm bear admitting it's Tired — the most honest planner stamp you will ever own.
One tiny bear, one firm OK — a mini Peppoko stamp for approving your own life choices in ink.
Stamp a small reading bear beside every book you finish — Peppoko's mini tribute to cozy chapters.
A studious little bear at 1.2 centimetres — mark study sessions with more charm than any highlighter.
Peppoko's chef bear, mini-stamp edition — for meal plans, recipe notes, and dinners that deserve documentation.
A tiny bear on official break duty — stamp it wherever your planner needs permission to rest.
Bath Time, declared by a very small bear — the mini stamp for tracking your most important self-care ritual.
A retro cream soda float, carved into a Peppoko rubber stamp — kissaten nostalgia in every press.
One stamp, pure celebration: Peppoko's Yaaaay! marks wins big and small with a soft, giddy flourish.
Peppoko makes doing nothing official — a Lazy Day stamp for the entries where the plan is no plan.
A girl-and-cat hello in stamp form — Peppoko's warmest greeting for letters, journals, and parcels.
Neko and Ebifurai no Shippo spin a full 360 on these paper clips — corner-dwellers keeping your papers politely in line.
Shirokuma and Tokage rotate to face whichever way your paperwork demands — Sumikko clips that make filing almost fun.
The Sumikko crew hosts a pajama party on this B5 clear file — a die-cut flap keeps your papers as snug as they are.
Rilakkuma lounges across a clear B5 pocket file — see your papers at a glance while a bear supervises the organising.
Sixty sheets of Sumikko friendship in four sticky designs — reminders delivered by characters who prefer the corner.
The Sumikko gang in pajamas across 60 sticky sheets — the gentlest possible format for a to-do list.
